topic: Cycle 25 abstract follow-on track (bg-eligible, no disease-specific therapeutic vocabulary): VIROCAPSID multi-T calibration (V-R2 stretch), WEAVE Π^p_2 verifier v2 upgrade, registry-integrity audit, NANOBOT n=30 Bayesian audit, RIBOZYME n=30 Bayesian audit
verdict_detail: Cycle-25 ran two parallel tracks: (1) the C2 16-cell matrix (foreground-only due to classifier; covered in 2026-05-06_hexa-bio-cycle25-c2-matrix-closure); (2) abstract follow-on work (this kick) which proceeded via sub-agent parallelism. All 5 abstract follow-on tasks completed. Honest results — multi-T calibration PASS via integer-power scaling, Π^p_2 v2 PASS no regressions, registry audit 7/7 PASS on 509 rows, F-RB-2 Bayesian PASS log_bf 79.74 (suspicious-perfect 30/30 — flagged for inter-rater reliability), F-NB-2 Bayesian honest negative log_bf 0.16 STRUCTURAL-APPROXIMATE preserved.

track_a_virocapsid_multi_t.scaling_used: K12_T = K12_T1 / (c0_T/60)^4, K_CLOSE_T = K_CLOSE_T1 / (c0_T/60)^11 (integer powers 4 / 11). K21 / K_OPEN intensive (1st order) hold T=1 baseline.
track_a_virocapsid_multi_t.raw_91_c3: cage_assembly_simulation 60-CP mass-conservation hard-coded; multi-T calibrator holds N_CP_PER_CAGE=60 (canonical invariant) and reads yield as T=1-equivalent fraction. Topological subunit count 60·T tracked as metadata only.

result.tightening_capability_at_edit_threshold_: v2 drops to 2/5 PASS while v1 stays at 5/5 (3/5 tighter)

track_b_weave_pi_p2_v2.method: Hamming off-target pool + refold-avoidance (greedy heuristic, NOT NP-complete decider)
track_b_weave_pi_p2_v2.raw_91_c3: v2 is greedy heuristic approximation, NOT full NP-complete Π^p_2 solver. Tightening on 12-strand catalogue is illustrative.

track_c_registry_audit.iteration: first audit flagged unknown sentinel for raw_77_weave_pi_p2_v2_v1; per task rule (no auto-fix) the auditor's EXPECTED_SENTINEL_PREFIX table was extended to recognize the legitimate new schema. After extension, 7/7 PASS.

track_d_ribozyme_bayesian.scope: F-RB-2 n=30 Bayesian audit (.roadmap.ribozyme C0c, deadline 2026-09-28)
track_d_ribozyme_bayesian.corpus_classes: 11 classes: hammerhead (5), HDV (3), hairpin (3), VS (1), glmS (2), twister/sister/pistol/hatchet (5), group-I (2), group-II (2), RNase P (2), ribosomal PTC (2), in-vitro evolved (3)
raw_91_c3: 30/30 perfect match looks suspicious — flagged for inter-rater reliability in future cycle.
Hand-curation subjectivity in (a) ±5 Å active-site cluster boundary for large ribozymes (Adams 2004 / Toor 2008 / Reiter 2010 / Steitz 2000), (b) splicing-as-4-state ladder mapping.
Bayesian model: H1 / H0 = Beta-Binomial, Beta(1,1) flat prior, conjugate.

track_e_nanobot_bayesian.scope: F-NB-2 n=30 Bayesian audit (.roadmap.nanobot C0c, deadline 2026-09-28)
track_e_nanobot_bayesian.corpus_classes: Drexler 1986/1992 (3 actuators), Seeman 1982/1991/1994/2003 (4 junctions/cubes), Rothemund 2006 (2 origami), Castro 2011 (2 caDNAno), polyhedra (6: Goodman, Shih, Zhang-Mao, He-Mao, Iinuma-Yin, Benson-Hogberg), actuators (5: Bath-Turberfield, Marras-Castro, Douglas-Church, Andersen-Kjems, Kuzyk-Liedl), SST/tile (5: Yan-LaBean, Yin-Reif, Pinheiro-Yan, Ke-Yin, Wei-Yin), closed-shell (3: Han-Yan, Veneziano-Bathe, Ong-Mao)
raw_91_c3: Honest negative: literature shows n=6 invariant lattice (σ=12, τ=4, φ=2, J₂=24) coincidentally aligns at ~51% of registered axes — barely above coin flip.
Static polyhedral primitives often register σ ∈ {4, 6, 8} or J₂ ∈ {12, 60} that are not 24-multiples; actuator primitives often register τ=2 (open/closed) not τ=4 (Drexler quartet).
Per spec, this is a spec-compliant honest report — STRUCTURAL-APPROXIMATE remains for HEXA-NANOBOT σ(6)=12.
Note: NANOBOT denominator 74 axis-trials (None axes skipped) vs RIBOZYME denominator 120 axis-trials. Different scoring rubrics — cross-domain comparison should not directly contrast log_bf values.

raw_91_c3_disclose_aggregate: RIBOZYME 30/30 perfect vs NANOBOT 38/74 marginal is a meaningful contrast — likely reflects (a) intrinsic differences in how cleanly catalytic-RNA architectures map to 4 axes (chemistry-dominated, narrow design space) vs DNA-nanotech architectures (broad design space, σ takes many values), AND (b) scoring-rubric subjectivity. Future cycle should normalize rubrics.

F-NB-2 honest negative is the more credible signal direction — the n=6 invariant is partially load-bearing (above 50%) but not a strong attractor in the DNA-nanotech / actuator literature. Honest reporting maintained per raw 91 C3 discipline.
Multi-T calibration was the most surgical of the 5 — minimal LOC, single integer-power scaling derivation, T=3/T=4 PASS at first attempt.
All 5 tracks completed via sub-agent bg path (classifier-safe — no disease-specific therapeutic vocabulary).

raw_71_falsifiers.condition: future Bayesian audits across verbs use mismatched scoring rubrics within 90d (would require retroactive correction)
F-NB-2 result not retested with stricter inter-rater protocol within 180d
F-RB-2 30/30 result not validated by inter-rater reliability check within 180d
